title: France records more than 5,700 excess deaths during June heatwave
summary: Figures come as large parts of southern Europe endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperaturesFrance recorded more than 5,700 excess deaths during its June heatwave, authorities have said, as swathes of southern Europe continue to endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperatures.The French national health agency said on Wednesday that it had recorded 5,764 excess deaths ⁠between ​17 June and 2 July, an excess mortality rate of 36%. title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
summary: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heatJapan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. title: Presentation: From Copy-Paste to Composition: Building Agents Like Real Software
summary: Jake Mannix discusses moving AI agents past chaotic "1970s BASIC" architectures. title: Why AI Needs a “Genie Coefficient”
summary: Major benchmarks measure what AI can do. None measure whether it does what you mean: the distance between what you ask an AI to do and the unspoken assumptions about how you want the AI to do it. We propose a new metric: the Genie coefficient.There’s often a gap between one person’s request and another’s understanding. Most of the time, we bridge it using general knowledge. title: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none of them use today; a majority intend to switch or add providers within the year, many within a quarter. Buying decisions turn on integration and total cost of ownership rather than headline token price — which is fortuna

title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents.
